testPurpose.objective: Verify eight isolated natural users can create, join, retain fixed identities, complete one full-deck Criminal Dance match through legal visible UI actions, preserve every private hand and draw boundary, and reach one cross-tab-consistent normalized terminal result.

successCriteria.description: All eight tabs retain distinct fixed player identities from room entry through the visible match terminal.
The host selects eight players with Inspector and Juvenile disabled, and every tab visibly renders the same full base-deck settings.
Exact hands, draws, transfers, forced choices, and actor-private results appear only on the owning tab and in the owning player's Observation.
Every strategic card, target, cancel, confirm, and forced response is selected autonomously by the owning player and is legal in that same player's visible UI.
Seat order, current player, hand counts, played piles, public cards, scores, and public log remain consistent across all eight tabs.
All eight tabs complete the score-to-10 match and display the same normalized terminal result regardless of winner, strategy quality, or match length.

testPurpose.recommendationRationale: Eight players is the Adapter maximum requested by the user and exercises the complete 32-card base deck. Natural-user mode forbids forced scenarios, so both optional expansions remain disabled and every strategic action stays autonomous. Fast removes only harness operation delay; Criminal Dance declares no scalable waits, so the resolved Server scale remains production-time 1.0.
